# TagzApp Copilot Instructions

## Project Overview

TagzApp is a comprehensive social media aggregation platform built with .NET 9, Blazor, and .NET Aspire. It collects and displays social media content from multiple providers (Twitter, YouTube, Mastodon, Twitch, Bluesky, etc.) based on hashtags, with moderation capabilities and real-time streaming.

## Core Architecture

- **Framework**: .NET 9 with global.json rollForward strategy
- **UI Framework**: Blazor Server and Blazor WebAssembly (hybrid approach)
- **Orchestration**: .NET Aspire for cloud-native application orchestration
- **Package Management**: Central Package Management enabled
- **Data Storage**: PostgreSQL and SQLite with Entity Framework Core
- **Real-time Communication**: SignalR hubs
- **Containerization**: Docker with docker-compose support

## Coding Standards & Conventions

### .NET Standards
- Follow Microsoft .NET coding conventions and naming standards
- Use nullable reference types consistently
- Implement proper async/await patterns throughout
- Use record types for DTOs and immutable data structures
- Implement IDisposable pattern correctly for resource management

### File Organization
```
src/
â”śâ”€â”€ TagzApp.Common/              # Shared contracts and models
â”śâ”€â”€ TagzApp.Common.Client/       # Client-shared models
â”śâ”€â”€ TagzApp.Abstractions/        # Core abstractions and interfaces
â”śâ”€â”€ TagzApp.Blazor/             # Main Blazor Server application
â”śâ”€â”€ TagzApp.Blazor.Client/      # Blazor WebAssembly client components
â”śâ”€â”€ TagzApp.Web/                # Web API endpoints
â”śâ”€â”€ TagzApp.AppHost/            # .NET Aspire orchestration
â”śâ”€â”€ TagzApp.ServiceDefaults/    # Shared service configurations
â”śâ”€â”€ TagzApp.ViewModels/         # UI data models
â”śâ”€â”€ TagzApp.Components/         # Reusable Blazor components
â”śâ”€â”€ TagzApp.Providers.*/        # Social media provider implementations
â”śâ”€â”€ TagzApp.Storage.*/          # Data persistence implementations
â”śâ”€â”€ TagzApp.Security/           # Authentication and authorization
â””â”€â”€ TagzApp.Communication/      # Messaging and pub/sub
```

### Naming Conventions
- **Classes**: PascalCase (e.g., `SocialMediaProvider`, `ContentModel`)
- **Interfaces**: IPascalCase (e.g., `ISocialMediaProvider`, `IMessagingService`)
- **Methods**: PascalCase (e.g., `GetContentForHashtag`, `StartAsync`)
- **Properties**: PascalCase (e.g., `DisplayName`, `ProviderId`)
- **Private fields**: _camelCase (e.g., `_httpClient`, `_configuration`)
- **Constants**: SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE or PascalCase for readonly
- **Razor components**: PascalCase.razor (e.g., `WaterfallMessage.razor`)

## Core Domain Models

### Content Model
```csharp
public class Content
{
    public long Id { get; set; }
    public required string Provider { get; set; }
    public required string ProviderId { get; set; }
    public string HashtagSought { get; set; } = string.Empty;
    public ContentType Type { get; set; } = ContentType.Message;
    public required Uri SourceUri { get; set; }
    public DateTimeOffset Timestamp { get; set; }
    public required Creator Author { get; set; }
    public string Text { get; set; } = string.Empty;
    public Card? PreviewCard { get; set; }
    public Emote[]? Emotes { get; set; }
}
```

### Creator Model
```csharp
public class Creator
{
    public string ProviderId { get; set; } = string.Empty;
    public string UserName { get; set; } = string.Empty;
    public string DisplayName { get; set; } = string.Empty;
    public required Uri ProfileUri { get; set; }
    public required Uri ProfileImageUri { get; set; }
}
```

### Hashtag Model
```csharp
public class Hashtag
{
    public string Text { get; set; } // Always includes # prefix
    public static string ClearFormatting(string text) // Removes # and lowercases
}
```

## Social Media Provider Pattern

### ISocialMediaProvider Interface
All social media providers must implement:
```csharp
public interface ISocialMediaProvider : IDisposable
{
    string Id { get; }
    string DisplayName { get; }
    string Description { get; }
    bool Enabled { get; }
    TimeSpan NewContentRetrievalFrequency { get; }
    
    Task<IEnumerable<Content>> GetContentForHashtag(Hashtag tag, DateTimeOffset since);
    Task<(SocialMediaStatus Status, string Message)> GetHealth();
    Task StartAsync();
    Task StopAsync();
    Task<IProviderConfiguration> GetConfiguration(IConfigureTagzApp configure);
    Task SaveConfiguration(IConfigureTagzApp configure, IProviderConfiguration providerConfiguration);
}
```

### Provider Implementation Guidelines
1. **Health Monitoring**: Always update `_Status` and `_StatusMessage` fields
2. **Error Handling**: Catch exceptions and return empty collections rather than throwing
3. **Rate Limiting**: Respect API rate limits and implement backoff strategies
4. **Content Mapping**: Map external APIs to the standard `Content` model
5. **Configuration**: Support enable/disable and provider-specific settings
6. **Async Patterns**: Use proper async/await throughout

### Provider Status Management
```csharp
public enum SocialMediaStatus
{
    Unknown,
    Healthy,
    Degraded,
    Unhealthy
}
```

## Blazor Components Guidelines

### Component Structure
- Use `@code` blocks for logic
- Implement `IDisposable` for SignalR connections and event subscriptions
- Use `@inject` for dependency injection
- Prefer record types for component parameters

### SignalR Integration
- Use `MessageHub` for real-time content streaming
- Use `ModerationHub` for moderation actions
- Always dispose SignalR connections in `Dispose()` method

### Styling
- Use Bootstrap classes for responsive design
- Custom CSS in `wwwroot/css/site.css`
- Provider-specific icons with consistent naming (e.g., `bi-twitter-x`, `icon-bluesky`)

## Data Access Patterns

### Entity Framework
- Use repository pattern for data access
- Implement both PostgreSQL and SQLite providers
- Use migrations for schema changes
- Async methods for all database operations

### Caching Strategy
- In-memory caching for frequently accessed data
- Content caching by hashtag
- Provider configuration caching

## Configuration Management

### appsettings.json Structure
```json
{
  "TagzApp": {
    "Moderation": {
      "Enabled": true
    },
    "Providers": {
      "Twitter": {
        "Enabled": false,
        "BearerToken": ""
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### Provider Configuration
- Each provider has its own configuration class
- Configuration UI components in `Components/Admin/`
- Support for enable/disable per provider

## Moderation System

### ModerationAction Model
```csharp
public class ModerationAction
{
    public ModerationState State { get; set; }
    public string? Reason { get; set; }
    public string? Moderator { get; set; }
    public DateTimeOffset? Timestamp { get; set; }
}
```

### Moderation States
- `Pending`: Awaiting moderation
- `Approved`: Content approved for display
- `Rejected`: Content rejected/hidden

## Testing Guidelines

### Unit Tests
- Use xUnit framework
- Test classes in `TagzApp.UnitTest` project
- Mock external dependencies using interfaces
- Test both success and failure scenarios

### Integration Tests
- Web tests in `TagzApp.WebTest` project
- Use `StubSocialMediaProvider` for testing
- Test SignalR hubs and real-time functionality

## Error Handling & Logging

### Exception Handling
- Use structured logging with `ILogger<T>`
- Catch and handle provider-specific exceptions
- Return empty collections instead of throwing in providers
- Log errors with appropriate log levels

### Health Checks
- Implement health checks for all providers
- Monitor database connectivity
- Report provider status in admin interface

## Security Considerations

### Authentication
- Support multiple OAuth providers (GitHub, LinkedIn, Google, Microsoft)
- Role-based authorization for moderation features
- Basic authentication for admin endpoints

### Content Safety
- Azure Content Safety integration for automatic moderation
- Configurable content filtering rules
- Emote handling and XSS prevention

## Performance Guidelines

### Content Retrieval
- Implement provider-specific retrieval frequencies
- Use background services for content polling
- Deduplicate content across providers
- Implement pagination for large result sets

### Real-time Updates
- Use SignalR for live content streaming
- Optimize for high-frequency updates
- Implement client-side masonry layout for dynamic content

## Development Workflow

### Adding New Providers
1. Create new project: `TagzApp.Providers.{ProviderName}`
2. Implement `ISocialMediaProvider` interface
3. Add provider models in `Models/` folder
4. Create configuration UI component
5. Register provider in DI container
6. Add provider icon and styling
7. Write unit tests

### Debugging
- Use .NET Aspire dashboard for service monitoring
- Check provider health status in admin interface
- Monitor SignalR connection status
- Use browser dev tools for client-side debugging

## Common Patterns

### Content Conversion
```csharp
// Converting external API models to Content
var content = new Content
{
    Provider = Id,
    ProviderId = externalItem.Id,
    Author = new Creator
    {
        DisplayName = externalItem.Author.Name,
        UserName = externalItem.Author.Username,
        ProfileUri = new Uri(externalItem.Author.ProfileUrl),
        ProfileImageUri = new Uri(externalItem.Author.AvatarUrl)
    },
    Text = externalItem.Message,
    Timestamp = externalItem.CreatedAt,
    SourceUri = new Uri(externalItem.Url),
    HashtagSought = tag.Text
};
```

### Emote Processing
```csharp
// For providers that support emotes (like Twitch)
public record Emote(int Pos, int Length, string ImageUrl);

// Format content with emotes in UI
public string FormatContentWithEmotes()
{
    if (!Emotes?.Any() ?? true) return Text;
    
    var formattedContent = Text;
    foreach (var emote in Emotes)
    {
        var emoteName = Text.Substring(emote.Pos, emote.Length);
        var emoteHtml = $"""<img class="emote" src="{emote.ImageUrl}" />""";
        formattedContent = formattedContent.Replace(emoteName, emoteHtml);
    }
    return formattedContent;
}
```

## Build and Deployment

### Local Development
- Use `dotnet run` in AppHost project
- Docker Compose for local services
- Hot reload enabled for Blazor components

### Production Deployment
- Container-based deployment
- Environment-specific configuration
- Health check endpoints for monitoring
- Graceful shutdown for providers

## Documentation Standards

### XML Documentation
- Document all public APIs
- Include parameter descriptions
- Document exceptions that may be thrown
- Use `<summary>`, `<param>`, `<returns>` tags

### Code Comments
- Explain complex business logic
- Document workarounds and temporary solutions
- Use TODO comments for known issues
- Explain non-obvious algorithms

---

When working on this codebase, always consider the multi-provider architecture, real-time requirements, and moderation capabilities. Maintain consistency with existing patterns and ensure proper error handling and logging throughout.
